planets.name: Sun
planets.description: The Sun is the star at the center of our Solar System. It's a nearly perfect sphere of hot plasma that provides the energy that sustains life on Earth.
funFacts: The Sun contains 99.86% of all mass in the Solar System
Light from the Sun takes about 8 minutes to reach Earth
The Sun's core temperature is about 15 million degrees Celsius
Over 1 million Earths could fit inside the Sun
The Sun is about 4.6 billion years old

planets.name: Mercury
planets.description: Mercury is the smallest planet in our solar system and closest to the Sun. Despite being closest to the Sun, it's not the hottest planet.
funFacts: Mercury has no atmosphere to retain heat
A day on Mercury is longer than its year
Mercury has the most cratered surface in the solar system
It's named after the Roman messenger god
Mercury can be seen without a telescope

planets.name: Venus
planets.description: Venus is the second planet from the Sun and the hottest planet in our solar system. It's often called Earth's twin due to similar size.
funFacts: Venus rotates backwards compared to other planets
It's the hottest planet despite not being closest to the Sun
A day on Venus is longer than its year
Venus has no moons
It's the brightest natural object in the night sky after the Moon

planets.name: Earth
planets.description: Earth is the third planet from the Sun and the only known planet to harbor life. It has liquid water on the surface and a protective atmosphere.
funFacts: Earth is the only planet not named after a Greek or Roman deity
70% of Earth's surface is covered by water
Earth's core is as hot as the Sun's surface
The Moon is slowly drifting away from Earth
Earth is the densest planet in the solar system

planets.name: Mars
planets.description: Mars is the fourth planet from the Sun and is often called the Red Planet due to its reddish appearance caused by iron oxide on its surface.
funFacts: Mars has the largest volcano in the solar system - Olympus Mons
Mars has two small moons: Phobos and Deimos
A day on Mars is almost the same length as Earth's
Mars has seasons like Earth
The sunset on Mars appears blue

planets.name: Jupiter
planets.description: Jupiter is the largest planet in our solar system. It's a gas giant with a famous Great Red Spot - a storm that has been raging for hundreds of years.
funFacts: Jupiter has at least 95 known moons
The Great Red Spot is a storm larger than Earth
Jupiter's magnetic field is 20,000 times stronger than Earth's
Jupiter acts as a cosmic vacuum cleaner, protecting inner planets
It has the shortest day of all planets

planets.name: Saturn
planets.description: Saturn is the sixth planet from the Sun and is famous for its spectacular ring system. It's the second-largest planet in our solar system.
funFacts: Saturn's rings are made of ice, rock, and dust
Saturn could float in water (if there was a bathtub big enough)
Saturn has 146 known moons
Its moon Titan has a thick atmosphere and liquid lakes
Saturn's rings span up to 282,000 km but are only about 10m thick

planets.name: Uranus
planets.description: Uranus is the seventh planet from the Sun and rotates on its side, making it unique among planets. It's an ice giant with a blue-green color.
funFacts: Uranus rotates on its side at a 98-degree angle
It was the first planet discovered with a telescope
Uranus has 27 known moons named after Shakespeare characters
Its blue color comes from methane in its atmosphere
Uranus has faint rings

planets.name: Neptune
planets.description: Neptune is the eighth and farthest planet from the Sun. It's an ice giant known for its striking blue color and powerful winds.
funFacts: Neptune has the strongest winds in the solar system (2,100 km/h)
It was predicted mathematically before being observed
Neptune has 16 known moons
Its largest moon, Triton, orbits backwards
Neptune takes 165 years to orbit the Sun once
